title: Utricularia blumei
taxonomy: Utricularia blumei is a species of bladderwort, a type of carnivorous plant that belongs to the family Lentibulariaceae. This family is a part of the order Lamiales, which includes other flowering plant families such as Acanthaceae and Plantaginaceae. The order Lamiales is a member of the clade Asterids, which is a group of flowering plants that also includes the orders Apiales and Dipsacales. The Asterids are part of the larger clade called the Angiosperms, which includes all flowering plants. The classification of Utricularia blumei reflects its unique characteristics as a carnivorous plant adapted to aquatic environments.

intro: Utricularia blumei, commonly known as Blume's bladderwort, is a carnivorous plant species belonging to the genus Utricularia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its expectorant properties, which help in relieving congestion and cough. The bioactive constituents of Utricularia blumei include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used in traditional medicine to prepare herbal teas, decoctions, and infusions, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues and other ailments.
